description: CBRE Group is a global commercial real estate services and investment firm that helps occupiers, owners, investors, and institutions buy, sell, lease, manage, operate, finance, and develop property.

overview.title: Business Scope
paragraphs: CBRE sits near the operating center of commercial real estate: brokerage and leasing, capital markets, property and facilities management, project management, workplace services, and real estate investment management. Its value proposition is scale, local market reach, and enough transaction and building data to make property feel a little less like folklore.

The company serves both occupiers and property owners, which gives it exposure to cyclical transaction activity as well as more recurring work tied to facilities, property operations, and long-term client outsourcing contracts.

overview.title: Operating Model
paragraphs: Historically, the repo context maps CBRE around Advisory & Transaction Services and Global Workplace Solutions. Recent company reporting has shifted toward segments such as Advisory Services, Building Operations & Experience, Project Management, and Real Estate Investments, reflecting the growing importance of outsourced building operations, flexible workplace offerings, and Turner & Townsend-led project work.

This makes CBRE less a single product company than a bundled infrastructure layer for corporate real estate. The customer buys advice, execution, and ongoing operations, ideally from the same giant machine that already knows where the elevators, leases, tenants, contractors, and invoices are.

overview.title: Free and Open Alternatives Lens
paragraphs: CBRE's core services do not map neatly to one open-source replacement, because much of its defensibility comes from human expertise, client relationships, proprietary market data, and global execution capacity. The relevant comparison space is therefore fragmented: open property data, cooperative real estate databases, open building-management tooling, open mapping, open ESG reporting workflows, and independent workplace analytics.

For Free The World, CBRE is best treated as a commercial real estate services platform whose closed advantages are data aggregation, procurement scale, and managed-service bundling. The open alternative story will likely be about decomposing those bundles into transparent data layers, interoperable facilities systems, and locally governed service networks.

sourceSuggestions.title: CBRE Investor Relations

sourceSuggestions.title: CBRE Services
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Official overview of CBRE's service lines and useful for mapping customer-facing products into registry categories.

sourceSuggestions.title: CBRE 2025 Annual Report
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary annual filing with updated business segment structure, risk factors, financials, and management discussion.

sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Authoritative filing history for verifying annual reports, quarterly updates, acquisitions, and segment changes.

sourceSuggestions.title: CBRE Corporate Responsibility Report

sourceSuggestions.title: CBRE Market Capitalization Snapshot

sourceSuggestions.title: CBRE Press Releases
